agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Nonprofits located in more populous ZIP codes may rely on large local donor bases and grassroots fundraising, resulting in lower professional fundraising fees.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Bielefeld (2000) shows that urban density and large population bases support localized grassroots fundraising structures.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant but weak negative correlation was found. Nonprofits in higher population ZIP codes spend slightly less on professional fundraising fees, indicating less reliance on external professional consulting where large local volunteer/donor networks are available.
